income tax purposes and  is not included as an item of tax preference in computing the federal alternative 
minimum tax for individuals.  Interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds is not exempt from present State

$19,285,000 Regional Water System Revenue Bonds, Series 2022 (Senior Lien)
Dated:  Date of Delivery  Due:  December 1, 2047
The Regional Water System Revenue Bonds, Series 2022 (Senior Lien) (the “
Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds”), 
of the Village of Oak Lawn, Cook County, Illinois (the “Village”), will be issued in fully registered form and will 
be registered initially only in the name of Cede & Co., as registered owner and nominee of The Depository Trust 
Company, New York, New York (“DTC”).  DTC will act as securities depository for the Series 2022 Senior Lien 
Bonds.  Ownership by the beneficial owners of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be evidenced by book‑entry 
only.  Payments of principal of and interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be made by Amalgamated 
Bank of Chicago, Chicago, Illinois, as bond registrar and paying agent, to DTC.  See “
Book -Entry  o nly  SyStEm ” herein.  
Individual purchases of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be made in the principal amount of $5,000 or any 
integral multiple thereof.
Interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds (computed on the basis of a 360‑day year consisting of twelve 
30‑day months) will be payable semi‑annually on each June 1 and December 1, commencing December 1, 2022, until 
the maturity or prior redemption thereof.
Proceeds of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be used to (a) refund certain outstanding obligations of the 
Village, (b) pay interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds through December 1, 2022, and (c) pay costs associated 
with the issuance of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds.  See “
USE of  ProcEEdS ” herein.
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are subject to redemption prior to maturity at the option of the Village, as a 
whole or in part, on any date on or after December 1, 2031, at the redemption price of par plus accrued interest to 
the redemption date.  See “
d EScriPtion  of  thE  SEriES 2022 SEnior  liEn BondS —Redemption” herein.
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are payable solely from the Net Revenues (as defined herein).  The Series 2022 
Senior Lien Bonds do not constitute an indebtedness of the Village within any constitutional or statutory limitation 
or provision or a pledge of the full faith and credit or the taxing power of the Village.  See “
SEcUrity  for  thE  SEriES 
2022 SEnior  liEn BondS  and  ProviSionS  of  thE  Bond  o rdinancE ” herein.
The scheduled payment of principal of and interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds when due will be 
guaranteed under a municipal bond insurance policy (the “Policy”) to be issued concurrently with the delivery of 
the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds by Build America Mutual Assurance Company (the “BAM”).  See “
Bond  inSUrancE ” 
and Appendix G herein.
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are offered when, as and if issued by the Village and received by Mesirow 
Financial, Inc., Chicago, Illinois (the “Underwriter”), subject to prior sale, to withdrawal or modification of the 
offer without notice, and to the approval of legality by Bond Counsel, and certain other conditions.  Chapman 
and Cutler LLP, Chicago, Illinois, will also act as Disclosure Counsel to the Village.  Peterson, Johnson & Murray 
Chicago, LLC, Chicago, Illinois, will pass on certain matters for the Village.  It is expected that beneficial interests 
in the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be available for delivery through the facilities of DTC on or about 
June 15, 2022.
The date of this Official Statement is May 25, 2022.

OFFICIAL STATEMENT 
Village of Oak Lawn 
Cook County, Illinois 
$19,285,000 Regional Water System Revenue Bonds, Series 2022 (Senior Lien) 
OVERVIEW 
The purpose of this Official Statement is to set forth certain information concerning the 
Village of Oak Lawn, Cook County, Illinois (the “Village” ), in connection with the offering and 
sale of its Regional Water System Revenue Bonds, Series 2022 (Senior L ien) (the “Series 2022 
Senior Lien Bonds”). The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are being issued by the Village pursuant 
to its powers as a home rule unit of government pursuant to Article VII, Section 6 of the Illinois 
Constitution of 1970 (the “Home Rule P owers”), a Master Bond Ordinance adopted by the 
President and Board of Trustees (the “Board” ) of the Village on the 8th day of July, 2014 (the 
“Master Bond Ordinance” ), as supplemented and amended to the date hereof, including as 
supplemented by an ordinance adopted by the Board on the 26th day of April, 2022 ( as 
supplemented by a Bond Order, the “Series 2022 Supplemental Ordinance” and, together with the 
Master Bond Ordinance, the “Bond Ordinance” ).  Capitalized terms used in this Official 
Statement and no t defined shall have the definitions ascribed to such terms in A PPENDIX A—
“DEFINITIONS UNDER THE MASTER BOND ORDINANCE”. 
The Village, by its Board, has provided for a municipally -owned regional waterworks 
utility (the “System” ) which serves wholesale custo mers (consisting of municipalities for 
municipal use and for resale by such municipalities to residents and others).  Specifically, the 
System serves municipal customers of the Village which purchase water from the Village pursuant 
to a Regional Water Agreement (as hereinafter defined), and  municipal customers of the Village 
which do not purchase water from the Village pursuant to a Regional Water Agreement, the same 
being the City of C ountry Club Hills, Illinois ( “Country Club Hills” ), the Village of Matteson, 
Illinois (“Matteson”), and the Village of Olympia Fields, Illinois (“Olympia Fields” and, together 
with Country Club Hills and Matteson, the “Southeast Water System Customers” ), of which 
Country Club Hills and Olympia Fields  have existing water contracts in place with the Village 
(collectively, the “Southeast Water Agreements” ).  The System also provides water to the 
municipally-owned local waterworks utility established by the Village for service to its retail 
customers (the “Local System” ).  The current Regional  Water Agreements  consist of sale, 
purchase and service agreements among ( i) the Village and the Village of Mokena, Illinois 
(“Mokena”), the Village of New Lenox, Illinois ( “New Lenox” ), the City of Oak Forest, Illinois 
(“Oak Forest” ), the Village of Orland Park, Illinois ( “Orland Park” ), and the Village of Tinley 
Park, Illinois (“Tinley Park” and, together with Mokena, New Lenox, Oak Forest and Orland Park, 
the “Southwest Water System Customers”), originally dated as of August 1, 2014, and as amended 
as of November 1, 2020 (the “Southwest System Regional Water Agreement”) and (ii) the Village 
and the Village of Chicago Ridge, Illinois ( “Chicago Ridge” ), the City of Palos Hil ls, Illinois 
(“Palos Hills” ), and the Village of Palos Park, Illinois (“Palos Park” and, together with Chicago 
Ridge and Palos Hills, the “North Water System Customers”  and, together with the Southwest 
Water System Customers, the “Regional Water System Customers” ), originally dated as of 
August 1, 2014, and as amended as of November  1, 2020 (the “North System Regional Water  
-2- 
Agreement” and, together with the Southwest System Regional Water Agreement, the “Regional 
Water Agreements”).  The current municipal customers of the System, the same being (i) the Local 
System, (ii) the Southeast System Customers, and (iii) the Regional Water System Customers, are 
collectively referred to herein as the “Municipal Customers”).  Tinley Park serves the Village of 
Orland Hills (“Orland Hills”), Mokena and New Lenox (the “Tinley Park System”).  Orland Hills 
is not a party to the Southwest System Regi onal Water Agreement and is not a Municipal 
Customer. 
 Each municipality that makes up the Municipal Customers currently owns and operates its 
own local water system (collectively with the Local System, the “Municipal Customer Water 
Systems”).   
The System delivers water from Lake Michigan purchased by the Village from the City of 
Chicago (the “City”) to the Local System, the Regional Water System Customers and the 
Southeast System Customers.  The Village purchases water for the System from the City pursuant 
to a Water Supply Agreement between the City and the Village, dated February 8, 2013 (the 
“Water Purchase Agreement”).  See “THE WATER PURCHASE AGREEMENT” herein. 
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are being issued by the Village to (i) refund a portion 
of tax-exempt portion of the Village’s outstanding Regional Water System Revenue Bonds, Tax-
Exempt/Taxable Revolving Line of  Credit, Series 2020A/2020B (the “2020 L/C” ) which was 
issued to pay a portion of the costs of certain improvements to the System (the “Project”), (ii) pay 
interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds through December 1, 2022 and (iii) pay the costs of 
the issuance of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds.  See “PLAN OF FINANCE” herein. 
At the time the Village entered into Regional Water Agreements with the Regional Water 
System Customers in 2013, the Municipal Customers determined that the capacity of the System 
was not adequate to serve the needs of the Mu nicipal Customers Water Systems through the year 
2030.  As a result, the Project was undertaken to  improve and expand the System in order to 
provide an adequate supply of water to the Munici pal Customers.  The Project consists of, but is 
not limited to, the installation of a Southwest Side Transmission Main and associated piping, 
valves, pump station improvements, standby e ngine generator improvements and electrical 
distribution and motor control equipment improvements, includ ing all legal, financial, 
engineering, advisory, Bond issuance and reserves and other reasonably related costs of providing 
and financing such improvements and expansions. 
The current estimated total cost of th e Project is $274,551,979.  As of March 1, 2022, 
approximately $121,000,000 has been spent to pay costs of the Project.  Expenditures to date have 
been financed through the issuance of (i) the 2020 L/C, issued as Senior Lien Bonds, and (ii) the 
Third Lien Bonds which were issu ed in evidence of various loan  agreements (collectively, the 
“Loan Agreements”) each between the Village and the Illinois Environmental Protection Agency 
(the “IEPA”).  The maximum authorized aggregat e principal amount of the 2020 L/C is 
$20,000,000, of which $18,122,538 is currently Outstandi ng.  The maximum aggregate principal 
amount of Loan Agreements executed by the Village is $158,215,000, of which $102,200,000 has 
been drawn and is Outstanding (as of April 1, 2022).  The remainder of the costs of the Project are 
expected to be financed through the issuance of additional Bonds, which will either be issued as  
-3- 
Senior Lien Bonds or Third Lien Bonds, the latter of which would evidence additional Loan 
Agreements executed with the IEPA.  The Village expects to complete the Project during Fiscal 
Year 2025. 
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are paya ble solely from the Net Revenues of the 
System.  See “SECURITY FOR THE SERIES 2022 SENIOR LIEN BONDS AND PROVISIONS OF THE BOND 
ORDINANCE” herein.  The Series 2022 Senior Lien B onds are on a parity and share ratably and 
equally in the Net Revenues with the 2020 L/C.  
The summaries of and references to all documents, statutes, reports and other instruments 
referred to in this Official Statement do not purport to be complete and are qualified in their entirety 
by reference to each such document, statute, report or instrument.   
This Official Statement contains “forward -looking statements” th at are based upon the 
Village’s current expectations and its projections about future events.  When used in this Official 
Statement, the words “project,” “estimate,” “i ntend,” “expect,” “sche duled,” “pro-forma” and 
similar words identify forward-looking statements .  Forward-looking statements are subject to 
known and unknown risks, uncertainties and factors that are outside of the control of the Village.  
Actual results could differ materially from those contemplated by the forward-looking statements.  
Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which 
speak only as of the date hereof.  Neither the Village nor any other party plans to issue any updates 
or revisions to these forward-looking statements based on future events.

LAN OF FINANCE 
THE REFUNDING 
A portion of the proceeds of the Seri es 2022 Senior Lien Bonds (the “Refunding 
Proceeds”) will be used to refund a portion of the Outstanding 2020 L/C.  The Refunding Proceeds 
will be deposited with Beverly Bank & Trust Company, N.A. (the “2020 L/C Bank”), on the date 
of the issuance of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds for the purpose of refunding such outstanding 
amounts.  The 2020 L/C will remain outstanding and may be used by the Village to draw additional 
amounts to pay costs of the Project prior to the issuance of additional Senior Lien Bonds or Third 
Lien Bonds to reimburse the Village for such costs.

DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2022 SENIOR LIEN BONDS 
GENERAL DESCRIPTION 
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be dated the date of issuance thereof, will be in 
fully registered form, without coupons, and will be in denominations of $5,000 or any integral 
multiple thereof under a book- entry only system operated by The Deposito ry Trust Company, 
New York, New York (“DTC”).  Principal of and interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds 
will be payable by Amalgamated Bank of Chicago, Chicago, Illinois (the “Registrar”). 
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will mature as shown on the inside cover page hereof.  
Interest on the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be payable each June 1 and December 1, 
commencing December 1, 2022. 
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will bear interest from their dated date, or from the 
most recent interest payment date to which interest has been paid or provided for, computed on 
the basis of a 360-day year consisting of twelve 30-day months.  The principal of the Series 2022 
Senior Lien Bonds will be payable in lawful money of the United States of America up on 
presentation and surrender thereof at the principal corporate trust office of the Registrar.  Interest 
on each Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond will be paid by check or draft of the Registrar payable upon 
presentation in lawful money of the United States of  America to the person in whose name such 
Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond is registered at the close of business on the15th day of the month 
next preceding the interest payment date.  
-5- 
REGISTRATION AND TRANSFER 
The Registrar will maintain books (the “Register” ) for the registration of ownership and 
transfer of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds.  Subject to the provisions of the Series 2022 Senior 
Lien Bonds as they relate to book- entry form, any Series 2022 Senior Lien B ond may be 
transferred upon the surrender thereof at the principal corporate trust office of the Registrar, 
together with an assignment duly executed by the registered owner or his or her attorney in such 
form as will be satisfactory to the Registrar.  No ser vice charge shall be made for any transfer or 
exchange of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds, but the Village or the Registrar may require payment 
of a sum sufficient to cover any tax or other governmental charge that may be imposed in 
connection with any transfer or exchange of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond s except in the case of 
the issuance of a Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond or Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds for the 
unredeemed portion of a Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond surrendered for redemption. 
The Registrar sh all not be required to transfer or exchange any Series 2022 Senior Lien 
Bond during the period beginning at the close of business on the 15th day of the month next 
preceding any interest payment date on such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond and ending at the 
opening of business on such interest payment date, nor to transfer or exchange any Series 2022 
Senior Lien Bond after notice calling such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond for redemption has been 
mailed, nor during a period of fifteen (15) days next preceding mailing of a notice of redemption 
of any Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds. 
R
EDEMPTION 
Optional Redemption.  The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are subject to redemption prior 
to maturity at the option of the Village as a whole or in part in integral multiples of $5,000 in any 
order of their maturity as determined by the Village  (less than all of the Series 2022 Senior Lien 
Bonds of a single maturity to be selected by the Registrar), on December 1, 2031, and on any date 
thereafter, at the redemption price of par plus accrued interest to the redemption date. 
Mandatory Sinking Fund Redemption.  The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are subject to 
mandatory redemption, in integral multiples of $5,000 selected by lot by the Registrar, at a 
redemption price of par plus accrued interest to the redemption date, on December 1  of the years 
and in the principal amounts as follows: 
Y
EAR PRINCIPAL AMOUNT 
2044 $2,150,000 
2045 3,435,000 
2046 6,715,000 
2047 (maturity) 6,985,000 
The principal amounts of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds to be mandatorily redeemed in 
each year may be reduced through the earlier optional redemption thereof, with any partial optional 
redemptions of such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds credited against future mandatory redemption 
requirements in such order of the mandatory redemption dates as the Village  may determine.  In  
-6- 
addition, on or prior to the 60th day preceding any mandatory redemption date, the Registrar may, 
and if directed by the Village shall, purchase Bonds required to be retired on such mandatory 
redemption date.  Any such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds so purchased shall be cancelled and 
the principal amount thereof shall be credited against the mandatory redemption required on such 
next mandatory redemption date. 
General.  The Village will, at least 30 days prior to any redemption date (unless a shorter 
time period shall be satisfactory to the Registrar), notify the Registrar of such redemption date and 
of the principal amount and maturity or maturities of Series 2 022 Senior Lien Bonds to be 
redeemed.  For purposes of any redemption of less than all of the outstanding Series 2022 Senior 
Lien Bonds of a single maturity, the particular Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds or portions of Series 
2022 Senior Lien Bonds to be re deemed shall be selected by lot by the Registrar from the Series 
2022 Senior Lien Bonds of such maturity by such method of lottery as the Registrar shall deem 
fair and appropriate (except when the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are held in a book -entry 
system, in which case the selection of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds to be redeemed will be made 
in accordance with procedures established by DTC or any other book-entry depository); provided 
that such lottery shall provide for the selection for redemption of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds 
or portions thereof in principal amounts of $5,000 and integral multiples thereof. 
Unless waived by any holder of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds to be redeemed, notice of 
the call for any redemption will be given by the Registr ar on behalf of the Village by mailing the 
redemption notice by first-class mail at least 20 days and not more than 60 days prior to the date 
fixed for redemption to each registered owner of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds to be redeemed 
at the address shown on the Register or at such other address as is furnished in writing by such 
registered owner to the Registrar. 
Unless moneys sufficient to pay the redemption price of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds 
to be redeemed at the option of the Village are received by the Registrar prior to the giving of such 
notice of redemption, such notice may, at the option of the Village, state that said redemption will 
be conditional upon the receipt of such moneys by the Registrar on or prior to the date fixed for 
redemption.  If such moneys are not received, such notice will be of no force and effect, the Village 
will not redeem such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds, and the Registrar will give notice, in the 
same manner in which the notice of redemption has been given, that such moneys were not so 
received and that such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will not be redeemed.  Otherwise, prior to 
any redemption date, the Village will deposit with the Registrar an amount of money sufficient to 
pay the redemption price of all the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds or portions of Series 2022 Senior 
Lien Bonds which are to be redeemed on that date. 
Subject to the provisions for a conditional redemption described above, notice of 
redemption having been given as described above and in the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bond  
Ordinance, and notwithstanding failure to receive such notice, the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds 
or portions of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds so to be redeemed will, on the redemption date, 
become due and payable at the redemption price therein specified, and from and after such date 
(unless the Village shall default in the payment of the redemption price) such Series 2022 Senior 
Lien Bonds or portions of Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds shall cease to bear interest.  Upon  
-7- 
surrender of such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds for redemption in accordance with said notice, 
such Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will be paid by the Registrar at the redemption price. 
SECURITY FOR THE SERIES 2022 SENIOR LIEN BONDS AND PROVISIONS OF THE BOND 
ORDINANCE  
GENERAL 
The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are revenue obligations of the Village.  The Series 
2022 Senior Lien Bonds are limited obligations of the Village which, together with the 2020 L/C 
and any Additional Bonds issued in the future, are payable from, and equally and ratably secured 
by, a pledge of the Net Revenues.  The Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds are limited obligations 
payable solely from the Net Revenues and do not constitute an indebtedness of the Village within 
the meaning of any constitutional or statutory limitation.   
In the Bond Ordinance, the Village grants the holders and registered owners of the Series  
2022 Senior Lien Bonds a security interest in the Net Revenues and in the funds and accounts held 
by the Village under the Bond Ordinance. 
Reference is made to A
PPENDIX B hereto for the proposed form of approving opinion of 
Chapman and Cutler LLP, Chicago, Illinois, Bond Counsel. 
For additional information regarding the 2020 L/C, see “—Parity Bonds — The 2020 L/C” 
herein. 
FLOW OF FUNDS 
All Revenues will be set aside as collected and be deposited into the Fund, which 
constitutes a trust fund for the sole purpose of carrying out the covenants, terms and conditions of 
the Bond Ordinance.  Amounts in the Fund will be used only in paying Operation and Maintenance 
Costs, paying the principal of and interest on all revenue bonds and other obligations of the Village 
which by their terms are payable from the Revenues derived from the System and providing for 
the establishment of and expenditure from the respective accounts as hereinafter described.  In the 
Bond Ordinance, the Village g rants to the holders and registered owners of Outstanding Bonds , 
including the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds,  a security interest in the Net Revenues and in the 
funds and accounts held by the Village under the Bond Ordinance. 
The Bond Ordinance maintains the following separate accounts in the Fund:  (i) the 
“Operation and Maintenance Account,” (ii) the “Operation and Maintenance Reserve Account,” 
(iii) the “Senior Lien Bond and Interest Account,”  (iv) the “Senior Lien Bond Reserve Account,”  
(v) the Junior Lien Bond and Interest Account,”  (vi) the “Junior Lien Bond Reserve Account,”  
(vii) the “Third Lien Bond and Interest Account,”  (viii) the “Third Lien Bond Reserve Account,”  
(ix) the “Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Account,”  and (x) the “Surplus A ccount.”  
The Bond Ordinance provides that each of these accounts will be credited each month or each 
quarter, as may be applicable for a receipt of Revenues under the Current Water Agreements, on  
-8- 
a given day or days as selected by the chief financial offi cer of the Village, without any further 
official action or direction, in the order in which said accounts are hereafter described, all moneys 
held in the Fund, in accordance with the following provisions. 
A. Operation and Maintenance Account .  There will be deposited to the credit 
of the Operation and Maintenance Account an amount sufficient, when added to the 
amount then on deposit in the Operation and Maintenance Account, to establish a balance 
to an amount not less than the amount necessary to pay Operation and Maintenance Costs 
for the then current period.  Amounts in the Operation and Maintenance Account will be 
used to pay Operation and Maintenance Costs. 
B. Operation and Maintenance Reserve Account.  For the purpose of providing 
for the payment of Operation and Maintenance Costs when there is a shortfall of funds in 
the Operation and Maintenance Account, there will be deposited to the credit of the 
Operation and Maintenance Reserve Account the amounts required at the times as provided 
in the Regional Water Agreement.  Upon any such shortfall of funds in the Operation and 
Maintenance Account, amounts in the Operation and Maintenance Reserve Account will 
be transferred by the Village, without any further order or direction, to the Operation and 
Maintenance Account to pay Operation and Maintenance Costs when due or as accrued. 
Notwithstanding any other provision of this Ordinance, the application of funds in the 
Operation and Maintenance Reserve Account, upon transfer to the Operation and 
Maintenance Account, to pay Operation and Maintenance Costs, is not a default or event 
of default under the Bond Ordinance.  
C. Senior Lien Bond and Interest Account.  There will next be deposited to the 
credit of the Senior Lien Bond and Interest Account and held, in cash and investments, the 
sums necessary timely to pay the Current Debt Service Requirement as due from the Senior 
Lien Bond and Interest Account, which deposits will be made fractionally on a quarterly 
basis. 
D. Senior Lien Bond Reserve Account .  At the time Senior Lien Bonds are 
issued, the Supplemental Ordinance or a related Bond Order for such Bonds may provide 
such requirement (a “Senior Lien Bond Debt Service Reserve Requirement”) for the Senior 
Lien Bond Reserve Account as determined at that time.  Amounts to the credit of the Senior 
Lien Bond Reserve Account will be used to pay principal of or interest on the Outstanding 
Senior Lien Bonds at any time when there are insufficient funds available in the Senior 
Lien Bond and Interest Account to pay the same and, further, to pay other amounts due 
from the Senior Lien Bond and Interest Account when insufficient funds are available 
therein, as and to the extent provided in the relevant instruments ( e.g., Credit Obligations 
or Interest Rate Exchange Transactions).   
Qualified Reserve Account Instruments may be provided in the Senior Lien Bond 
Reserve Account in lieu of cash. 
The Senior Lien Bond Debt Service Reserve Requirement with respect to the Series 
2022 Senior Lien Bonds is $0.  
-9- 
E. Junior Lien Bond and Interest Account.  There will next be deposited to the 
credit of the Junior Lien Bond and Interest Account and held, in cash and investments, the 
sums necessary timely to pay the Current Debt Service Requirement as due from the Junior 
Lien Bond and Interest  Account, which deposits will be made fractionally (monthly or 
quarterly or otherwise) as set forth in a Supplemental Ordinance and otherwise as may be 
reasonably determined by the chief financial officer of the Village. 
F. Junior Lien Bond Reser ve Account .  At the time Junior Lien Bonds are 
issued, the Supplemental Ordinance or a related Bond Order for such Bonds may provide 
such requirement for the Junior Lien Bond Reserve  Account as determined at that time.  
Amounts to the credit of the Bond Re serve Account will be used to pay principal of or 
interest on the Outstanding Junior Lien Bonds at any time when there are insufficient funds 
available in the Junior Lien Bond and Interest Account to pay the same and, further, to pay 
other amounts due from the Junior Lien Bond and Interest Account when insufficient funds 
are available therein, as and to the extent provided in the relevant instruments (e.g., Credit 
Obligations or Interest Rate Exchange Transactions).   
Qualified Reserve Account Instruments m ay be provided in the Junior Lien Bond 
Reserve Account in lieu of cash. 
G. Third Lien Bond and Interest Account.  There will next be deposited to the 
credit of the Third Lien Bond and Interest Account and held, in cash and investments, the 
sums necessary timely to pay the Current Debt Service Requirement as due from the Third 
Lien Bond and Interest Account, which deposits will be made fractionally (monthly or 
quarterly or otherwise) as set forth in a Supplemental Ordinance and otherwise as may be 
reasonably determined by the chief financial officer of the Village. 
H. Third Lien Bond Reserve Account.  At the time Third Lien Bonds are issued, 
the Supplemental Ordinance or a related Bond Order for such Bonds may provide such 
requirement for the Third Lien Bond Reserve Account as determined at that time.  Amounts 
to the credit of the Third Lien Bond Reserve Account will be used to pay principal of or 
interest on the Outstanding Third Lien Bonds at any time when there are insufficient funds 
available in the Third Lien Bond and Interest Account to pay the same and, further, to pay 
other amounts due from the Third Lien Bond and Interest Account when insufficient funds 
are available therein, as and to the extent provided in the relevant instruments (e.g., Credit 
Obligations or Interest Rate Exchange Transactions).   
Qualified Reserve Account Instruments may be provided in the Third Lien Bond 
Reserve Account in lieu of cash. 
I. Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Account .  There will next be 
credited to the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Account and held, in cash and 
investments, such amount as is required pursuant to the Regional Water Agreement and 
such further amounts, as may be available, which are in the reasonable judgment of the 
Board advisable for the purposes of the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve 
Account.  
-10- 
Amounts to the credit of  the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Account 
will be used, subject to the requirements of the Regional Water Agreement, for (i)  the 
payment of the cost of extraordinary maintenance, necessary repairs and replacements, or 
contingencies, or for improvements, repairs or replacements to the System required by any 
agency of the State of Illinois or the United States Government, the payment for which no 
other funds are available, in order that the System may at all times be able to render efficient 
service and (ii)  the payment of principal of or interest on any Outstanding Bonds at any 
time when there are no other funds available for that purpose in order to prevent a default 
and shall be transferred to the appropriate Bond and Interest Account for such purpose.  
Whenever an amount is withdrawn from the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve 
Account for the purpose stated in clause  (ii) of the  preceding sentence, the amount so 
transferred will be added to the amount to be next and thereafter credited to the Renewal, 
Repair and Replacement Reserve Account until full reimbursement to the Renewal, Repair 
and Replacement Reserve Account has been made. 
J. Surplus Account.  All moneys remaining in the Fund, after crediting the 
required amounts to the respective accounts described in paragraphs A through I of this 
section, and after making up any deficiency in  such accounts will be credited each month 
to the Surplus Account.  Funds in the Surplus Account will first be used to make up any 
subsequent deficiencies in any of the accounts hereinabove named and then, at the 
discretion of the Board or as covenanted by the Village in the Regional Water Agreemen t 
or in Supplemental Ordinances related to any issue of Parity Bonds or other obligations, 
including subordinate revenue obligations payable from the Revenues, will be used for one 
or more of the following purposes without any priority among them except as  may be 
covenanted: 
1. For the purpose of constructing or acquiring repairs, replacements, 
or improvements or extensions to the System. 
2. For the purpose of calling and redeeming Outstanding Bonds which 
are callable at the time or for the purpose of purchasing Outstanding Bonds. 
3. For the purpose of paying principal of and interest on any bonds or 
other obligations issued for the purpose of acquiring or constructing repairs, 
replacements, or improvements or extensions to the System or otherwise properly 
payable from the Fund or refunding obligations issued for such purposes. 
4. For transfer to the general funds of the Village as payment to the 
Village in return for the Village’s operation of the System (the “Equitable Return” 
as defined, described and calculated pursuant to the terms of the Regional Water 
Agreements). 
5. For any other lawful corporate purpose relating to the System.  
-11- 
Any amounts to the credit of the accounts of the Fund in excess of the then current 
maximum requirement therefor will be trans ferred not less often than annually by the Village 
officer charged with accounting for the Fund to the Surplus Account. 
Moneys to the credit of the Fund and all accounts within the Fund may be invested in 
Qualified Investments.   Such investments may be sold from time to time in accordance with 
Village procedures as funds may be needed for the purpose for which said respective accounts 
have been created.  Investments in the accounts  must mature or be subject to redemption at the 
option of the holder thereof prior to the time when needed, and, in any event, within the times as 
follows: 
ACCOUNT TIME 
Operation and Maintenance 6 months 
Bond and Interest 1 year 
Bond Reserve 5 years 
Renewal, Repair and Replacement 
Reserve 
 
5 years 
Surplus 5 years 
provided, however, that amounts on deposit in any Bond and Interest Account intended to be 
applied to interest due beyond a period of one year (such as interest capitalized from bond 
proceeds) may be invested to the time expected to be paid out for such purpose. 
All interest or profit earned on any funds so invested in a Bond and Interest Account will 
be retained therein; and all interest or profit earned on any funds so invested in the  related Bond 
Reserve Account will be credited upon receipt to the corresponding Bond and Interest Account.  
The Village may take credit for such earnings in any monthly accounting after receipt.  All interest 
or profit earned on any funds so invested in other accounts of the Fund will be credited to the Fund 
generally. 
For the purpose of determining whether sufficient cash and investments are on deposit in 
such accounts under the requirements of the Bond Ordinance, investments will be valued as of the 
close of each Fiscal Year in the manner as follows:  If in a Bond Reserve Account or in the 
Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Account, at the market value, and if in any other 
account of the Fund, at amortized cost.  
P
ARITY BONDS 
Issuance of Parity Bonds Under the Bond Ordinance.  So long as there is no default existing 
at the time of issuance, Bonds of any given lien priority and not to exceed the sum of $297,000,000, 
plus any additional amounts necessary to provide for the refunding of said initial amount of 
$297,000,000 Bonds, may be issued pursuant to the terms of the Bond Ordinance without any 
further requirements.  No other obligations or bonds of any kind may be issued which are payable 
from the Revenues except upon compliance with the conditions set out below.  
-12- 
Parity Bonds may be issued for the purpose of paying any lawful cost of the System, 
including, without limitation, the cost of repairs, replacements, renewals, improvements and 
extensions to the System, for prepayments of the cost of purchased power or other Operations and 
Maintenance Costs, termination fees related to Interest Rate Exchange Obligations or hedge fees 
relating to same or to Bonds or for refunding Outstanding Bonds upon compliance with the 
following conditions: 
1. The amounts required to be credited to the Operation and Maintenance 
Account, Operation and Maintenance Reserve Account, Senior Lien Bond and Interest 
Account and Senior Lien Bond Reserve Account under the Bond Ordinance must have 
been credited in full up to the date of the delivery of such Parity Bonds, which fact may be 
proved by a certificate of the President of the Village, countersigned by the Village 
Treasurer or Director of Finance (or if such officers are then vacant, by the persons acting 
in such capacities). 
2. The Village will have obtain ed the opinion of an attorney or firm of 
attorneys whose opinions are routinely accepted by purchasers of municipal bonds in the 
national market for same stating:  (a)  that such Bonds are valid and binding special 
obligations of the Village payable from Re venues and (b)  that the Regional Water 
Agreement is a valid and binding obligation of the Village and the Regional System 
Customers and the Southeast System Customers, if applicable, and that the payments to be 
made to the Village pursuant to the Regional Water Agreement will constitute Revenues 
available, in accordance with the terms of the Regional Water Agreement and the Bond 
Ordinance, to pay such Bonds.  Such opinion of counsel may be limited by reference to 
review of such proceedings and documents and as subject to limitations of bankruptcy and 
insolvency laws, rules of equity, and the like as are customary; such opinion limitations to 
conclusively deemed acceptable by acceptance by the Village. 
3. The Village shall not issue any Parity Bonds unless the Net Revenues of the 
System as shown by the most recently available audit of an independent certified public 
accountant or the Adjusted Net Revenues (as hereinafter defined ) of the System for such 
year equals at least 125% of Maximum Annual Debt Service, computed immediately after 
the issuance of the proposed Parity Bonds, but only for those Fiscal Years in which the 
Outstanding Bonds immediately prior to such issuance will continue to be Outstanding 
Bonds as provided herein (the “ Additional Bonds Coverage  Requirement”); provided, 
however, that for the purpose of determining the Maximum Annual Debt Service, the 
principal requirement of the Outstanding Bonds shall include only such amounts of the 
2020 L/C not expected to be refinanced with IEPA proceeds within 60 days of issuance 
divided by the number of years of the repayment term, and that the interest requirement on 
the Outstanding 2020 L/C shall be calculated at the Assumed Variable Rate. 
If necessary, the Village covenants to increase fees, charges, and rates for the use and 
service of the System to a level such that the increased Net Revenues (the “ Adjusted Net 
Revenues”) will be sufficient to provide the Additional Bonds Coverage Requirement, as 
evidenced by the certificate of an independent consulting engineer, an independent certified public  
-13- 
accountant or an independent financial consultant employed for that purpose, which certificate 
shall be filed with and approved by the Village prior to the issuance of the proposed Parity Bonds. 
The reference to and acceptance of an audit, or an adjusted statement of the Net Revenues 
and certificate, as the case may be, and the determination of the Board of the sufficiency of Net 
Revenues shall be conclusive evidence that the aforementioned conditions have been met and that 
the Parity Bonds are properly issued hereunder; and no right to challenge such determination is 
granted to the registered owners of the Bonds. 
The 2020 L/C .  The 2020 L/C constitutes a Parity Bond with respect to the Series 2022 
Senior Lien Bonds.  The Village established the 2020 L/C provide the liquidity necessary to fund 
the costs of the Project temporarily until longer -term financing can be secured to finance such 
Project costs.  The 2020 L/C is a revolving line of credit.   
The 2020 L/C is governed by the terms of a Continuing Covenant Agreement, dated 
April 1, 2020, between the Village and the 2020 L/C Bank (the “2020 L/C CCA”).   The following 
paragraphs provide a summary of certain provisions of the 2020 L/C and the 2020 L/C CCA , 
provided, that such summary expressly does not endeavor to describe all provisions of the 2020 
L/C CCA and is qualified in all respects by reference to the actual provisions of the 2020 L/C 
CCA, a copy of which is available from the Village.  
The maximum principal amount authorized under the 2020 L/C is currently $20,000,000 
(the “Maximum 2020 L/C Principal Amount”) of which $18,122,538 is currently Outstanding.  A 
portion of the proceeds of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds will pay a portion of such 
Outstanding amount, however, the Village’s authorization to borrow under the 2020 L/C will 
remain after the issuance of the Series 2022 Senior Lien Bonds to pay for additional Project costs.  
The Village and the 2020 L/C Bank have agreed to modify the terms of the 2020 L/C to, among 
other things, reduce the Maximum 2020 L/C Principal Amount  to $13,000,000 (the “2022 
Modifications”). 
The 2020 L/C CCA provides that the 2020 L/C Bank’s obligation to provide credit to the 
Village under the 2020 L/C terminates on April 1, 2025 (the “2020 L/C Termination Date”).  
Amounts Outstanding under the 2020 L/C bear interest at variable interest rates determined 
by adding a basis point spread, which differs based on whether the Outstanding amounts are 
taxable (the “Taxable Rate” ) or tax-exempt to the one -month LIBOR (as described in the 2020 
L/C CCA).  The 2022 Modifications would amend the 2020 L/C CCA to change the basis for such 
rates from LIBOR to Ameribor.  Interest on the 2020 L/C is payable on the first day of each month.  
The 2020 L/C also requires the Village to pay a commitment fee equal to 0.00833% of the 
difference between the Maximum 2020 L/C Principal Amount and the amount then-Outstanding 
under the 2020 L/C on the first day of each month. 
The 2020 L/C includes various provisions which could increase the amounts to be paid by 
the Village under the 2020 L/C.  Among these, the Village may become obligated to pay additional 
amounts to the 2020 L/C Bank if certain circumstances (i) require the payment of additional taxes 
by the 2020 L/C Bank or changes the basis of tax payments with respect to the 2020 L/C, (ii)  
-14- 
impose or increase or deem appli cable any reserve, liquidity ratio, assessment, insurance charge, 
special deposit or other similar requirement against assets of, deposits with or for the account of, 
or credit extended by, the 2020 L/C Bank (or any future holder of the bonds issued thereunder) (a 
“2020 L/C Bondholder”), (iii) impose any other condition on the 2020 L/C Bank or the 2020 L/C 
Bondholder which increases the cost of the 2020 L/C Bank or 2020 L/C Bondholder under the 
2020 L/C, or (iv) increase the amount of capital required to be  maintained by the 2020 L/C Bank 
or any 2020 L/C Bondholder.  In addition, the 2020 L/C CCA provides that the 2020 L/C will bear 
interest at a default rate equal to the Taxable Rate plus 2.00%  upon the occurrence of an event of 
default under the 2020 L/C.   
The 2020 L/C provi des that if the amount payable under the 2020 L/C for any period 
exceeds the amount of interest that would have been payable for such period if interest had been 
calculated at the Maximum Interest Rate, then interest for such period is payable in an amount 
calculated at the Maximum Interest Rate.  “Maximum Interest Rate” means the rate which is equal 
to 9% per annum (13 ½% for taxable amounts) or 125% (200% for taxable amounts) of the rate 
for the most recent date shown in the 20 G.O. Bo nds Index of average municipal bond yields as 
published in the most recent edition of The Bond Buyer, published in New York, New York (or 
any successor publication or index, or if such publication or index is no longer published, then any 
index of long term municipal tax-exempt bond yields then selected by the Board), at the time of 
the most recent change in the rate borne by the 2020 L/C.  However, if any amount is not paid 
under the 2020 L/C because of the application of the Maximum Interest Rate (the “Excess Interest 
Amount”), then the principal amount with respect to which interest is payable will bear interest at 
the Maximum Interest Rate until the Excess Interest Amount is paid. 
Upon the occurrence of an event of default under the 2020 L/C CCA for nonpayment of 
the principal of or interest on the 2020 L/C or any Parity Bond or a default in the observance or 
performance of any agreement or condition relating to or evidencing any Parity, the 2020 L/C 
Bank may request that the Village issue refunding bonds in order to make such unpaid payments.  
The Village is required by the 2020 L/C CCA to, upon receipt of such request, authorize and offer 
for sale such refunding bonds in an amount sufficient to pay the unpaid principal of and interest 
within 120 days after such request.   
C
OVENANTS OF THE VILLAGE 
In the Bond Ordinance, the Village covenants and agrees with the holders and registered 
owners of the Outstanding Bonds as follows: 
A. The Village will proceed with due diligence to construct the Project, will 
maintain the System in good repair and working order, will operate the System efficiently 
and faithfully, and will punctually perform all duties with respect thereto  required by the 
Constitution and laws of the State of Illinois and the United States of America. 
B. The Village will establish and maintain at all times reasonable fees, charges, 
and rates for the use and service of the System and will provide for the col lection thereof 
and the segregation and application of the Revenues in the manner provided by the Bond 
Ordinance, sufficient at all times to pay Operation and Maintenance Costs, to pay the  
-15- 
principal of and interest on all revenue bonds or other obligations of the Village which by 
their terms are payable solely or otherwise from the Revenues, and to provide Net 
Revenues in each Fiscal Year in the amount of not less than the Current Debt Service 
Requirement for all Outstanding Bonds and other obligations payable from the Senior Lien 
Bond and Interest Account, the Junior Lien Bond and Interest Account, and the Third Lien 
Bond and Interest Account  for such Fiscal Year; and to provide such additional amounts 
as may be required to provide for the creation and maintenance of the respective accounts 
of the Fund. 
C. The Village from time to time will make all needful and proper repairs, 
replacements, additions, and betterments to the System so that it may at all times be 
operated properly and advantageously; and when any necessary equipment or facility has  
been worn out, destroyed, or otherwise is insufficient for proper use, it will be promptly 
replaced so that the value and efficiency of the System will be at all times fully maintained. 
D. The Village will establish such rules and regulations for the control and 
operation of the System as are necessary for the safe, lawful, efficient and economical 
operation thereof. 
E. The Village will make and keep proper books and accounts (separate and 
apart from all other records and accounts of said Village), in which complete entries will 
be made of all transactions relating to the System, and  will provide for an audit of the 
System accounts in accordance with State law and any other lawful requirements or as may 
be agreed pursuant to a Supplemental Ordinance. 
F. The Village will keep the books and accounts for the System in accordance 
with generally accepted fund reporting practices for municipal enterprise funds; provided, 
however, that the periodic credits to the Senior Lien Bond and Interest Account, the Junior 
Lien Bond and Interest Account, the Third Lien Bond and Interest Account, the Senior 
Lien Bond Reserve Account, the Junior Lien Bond Reserve Account the Third Lien Bond 
Reserve Account and the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Account will be in 
cash or cash equivalents, and said funds will be accounted for in cash and investments. 
G. The Village may sell or dispose of any property of the System only when 
such sale or disposal is in the judgment of the Village advisable for the best interests of the 
Village and the System and which will not impair the ability of the System to provide 
service under the Regional Water Agreements and to produce the Net Revenues sufficient 
to meet the covenant described in paragraph B of this heading ; provided, however, that if 
such sale either at one time or pursuant to a course of action will result in disposition of 
10% or more of the System as valued by gross book value of assets or outstanding debt 
(whichever is less), then the Village must obtain a report of an independent utility 
professional concurring in the Village’s judgment as to no impairment of Net Revenues; 
and the Village will take no action in relation to the System which would unfavorably affect 
the security of the Outstanding Bonds and other obligations payable or the prompt payment 
of the principal thereof and interest thereon.  Any amounts received from the sale of 
property of the System will be deposited to the credit of the Surplus Account.  
-16- 
H. Any holder or registered owner of a Bond may proceed by civil action to 
compel performance of all duties required by law and the Bond Ordinance, including the 
making and collecting of sufficient charges and rates for the service supplied by the System 
and the application of the income and revenue therefrom. 
I. The Village will carry insurance on the System of the kinds and in the 
amounts which are usually carried by private parties operating similar properties, covering 
such risks as shall be recommended by a competent insurance consultant employed by the 
Village for the purpose of making such recommendations.  Alternatively the Village will 
self-insure or provide insurance by means of a self-insurance pool with other units of local 
government, provided that either private insurance is not available at a reasonable cost or 
the Village or the pool will have assets or will have created a self-insurance reserve fund 
in an amount as will be determined by a competent insurance consultant to be not 
unreasonable in view of the risks insured.  All moneys received for property loss under 
such insurance policies or from the self-insurance reserve fund or pool will be deposited to 
the credit of the fund or account from which paid and used in making good the loss or 
damage in respect of which they were paid, either by repairing the property damaged or 
making replacement of the property destroyed, and provision for making good such loss or 
damage shall be made within 90 days from the date of the loss.  The proceeds derived from 
any and all policies for workers’ compensation or public liability or from the self-insurance 
reserve fund or pool will be paid into the Operation and Maintenance Account and used in 
paying the claims on account of which they were received or will be used to reimburse any 
account from which the claim was previously paid.  The payment of premiums, regular 
payments to a pool and regular accumulations for a reserve fund required under the 
provisions of this covenant will be considered an Operation and Maintenance Cost.  
J. The Village covenants not to provide any free service of the System, and, 
to the extent permitted by law, the Village will not grant a franchise for the operation of 
any competing water system within the Village. 
K. The Village will adopt a budget for the Fund prior to the beginning of each 
Fiscal Year, subject to applicable State law, providing for Revenues sufficient for payment 
of all sums to be required in the Fiscal Year so as to comply with the terms of the Bond 
Ordinance and of any long-term water supply or other agreements related to the System.  
In any such budget, the Village may allocate unencumbered moneys in the Surplus Account 
as available to meet Revenue requirements for such Fiscal Year, and if the Village makes  
such allocation, then the Village will cause such moneys to be transferred to the Revenue 
Fund generally at the start of such Fiscal Year and treated therein as Revenues, subject that 
until all requirements for deposit into the accounts of the Fund under the Bond Ordinance 
for such Fiscal Year have been fully met, none of such allocated moneys may be deposited 
(again) to the credit of the Surplus Account. 
L. The Village covenants that it will not issue any bonds, limited obligations 
or other evidences of indebtedness, except pursuant to the provisions of this Ordinance, 
secured by a pledge of or claim upon the income or any part of the Revenues; that the 
Village will not create or suffer to be created any lien or charge upon the System or any  
-17- 
part thereof or upon the Revenues except the lien and charge of the Bonds secured hereby 
upon such Revenues, or such other bonds or obligations that may be issued pursuant to the 
provisions of this Ordinance; and that, from such Revenues or other available funds, it will 
pay or cause to be discharged, or will make adequate provisions to satisfy and discharge, 
within 90 days after the same shall become payable, all lawful claims and demands for 
labor, materials, supplies or other objects which, if unpaid, might by law become a li en 
upon the System or any portion thereof or the Revenues therefrom; provided, however, that 
the Bond Ordinance does not require the Village to pay or cause to be discharged, or make 
provision, for, any such lien or charge so long as the validity thereof is contested in good 
faith and by appropriate legal proceedings which stay the enforcement thereof. 
M. The Village will at all times maintain its corporate existence and will do and 
perform or cause to be done and performed all acts and things required to be done or 
performed by or on behalf of the Village under the provisions of applicable law and the 
Bond Ordinance. 
N. The Village will not do, or omit to do, or suffer to be done or omitted to be 
done, any matter or thing whatsoever whereby the pledge of or lien of the Bonds on the 
Revenues or any part thereof might or could be lost or impaired. 
O. The Village will require all payments to be made to the Village as charges 
for services from the System to be made in lawful money of the United States of America 
and will not accept as payment any of the Bonds or any other obligation or security. 
P. The Village will cause all necessary filings  of the Bond Ordinance, or 
amendments or supplements, or other documents necessary or advisable to perfect and 
preserve the rights of the Bondholders. 
Q. The Village covenants and agrees to fulfill every covenant, condition and 
requirement of the Southeast  Water Agreements, as may be applicable, and the Regional 
Water Agreement, to maintain said agreements as enforceable, not to alter or amend said 
agreements in any manner detrimental to the Bondholders, and to enforce said contracts 
according to the tenor thereof for the benefit and security of the Bondholders.  This 
covenant is made solely for the benefit of the Bondholders. 
R. The Village covenants and agrees with the holders and registered owners of 
the Outstanding Bonds that the Village will establish and maintain at all times reasonable 
fees, charges, and rates for the use and service of the System and will provide for the 
collection thereof and the segregation and application of the Revenues in the manner 
provided by the Bond Ordinance, sufficient to provide Net Revenues in each Fiscal Year 
in the amount of not less than 125% of the Current Debt Service Requirement for all 
Outstanding Bonds and other obligations payable from the Senior Lien Bond and Interest 
Account for such Fiscal Year, provided, however, that such coverage requirement shall not 
apply to any accelerated payments.    
-18- 
PROVISIONS OF BOND ORDINANCE A CONTRACT 
The provisions of the Bond  Ordinance constitute a contract between the Village and the 
holders and registered owners of the Outstanding Bonds; and no changes, additions, or alterations 
of any kind will be made to the Bond Ordinance so long as there are any Outstanding Bonds , 
except as described under “—A MENDMENT”. 
DEFEASANCE 
Bonds which are no longer Outstanding Bonds will cease to have any lien on or right to 
receive or be paid from Revenues and will no longer have the benefits of any covenant for the 
holders or registered owners of Outstanding Bonds as set forth in the Bond Ordinance. 
AMENDMENT 
The rights and obligations of the Village and of the registered owners of Outstanding Bonds 
may from time to time be modified or amended by a supplemental ordinance adopted by the Board 
with the written consent of the registered owners of not less than fifty -one percent (51%) of the 
principal amount of all Outstanding Bonds affected (excluding any of said bonds owned by or 
under the control of the Village); provided, however, that no such modification or amendment will 
extend or change the maturity of or date of redemption prior to maturity, or reduce the interest rate 
on, or permit the creation of a preference or priority of any Outstanding Bond or Outstanding 
Bonds over any other Outstanding Bond or Outstanding Bonds, or otherwise alter or impair the 
obligation of the Village to pay the principal of, interest on and redemption premium (if any) on 
any of the Outstanding Bonds at the time, place, rate, and in the currency provided therein, or alter 
or impair the obligations of the Village with respect to the t ax-exempt status, the registration, 
transfer, exchange or notice of redemption of Bonds, without the express consent of the registered 
owners of all the Outstanding Bonds affected; nor may any such modification or amendment 
reduce the percentage of the regis tered owners of Outstanding Bonds required for the written 
consent of such modification or amendment without the consent of the owners of all of the 
Outstanding Bonds.  If a given Series of Bonds is fully and irrevocably insured or otherwise 
provided for as to the timely payment of principal and interest by a bond insurance policy, a letter 
of credit, or some other means, and such policy provider or letter of credit provider shall not be in 
default, then any consent to amendment as provided in the Bond Ordi nance will not be given by 
the owners of Bonds of such Series, but rather will be obtained from such provider, whose consent 
may or may not be given in its complete discretion, and whose consent will be binding on such 
owners and all successors in interest.   
In obtaining or receiving the consents of registered owners, the Village may establish 
reasonable rules of procedure including, without limitation, rules relating to (i) a record date to fix 
the registered owners who are entitled to vote, (ii) solicitation of proxies and (iii) a meeting of the 
registered owners for the taking of actions.  The registered owners of Bonds may vote their Bond 
interest in fractional shares.  In the event that Bonds are registered in the name or names of 
nominees or depositories, consent of such owners by proxy in accordance with the applicable 
customs of the securities industry or rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the  
-19- 
“SEC”), Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (“MSRB”) or other association or agency having 
jurisdiction will be sufficient.   
THE WATER PURCHASE AGREEMENT  
The following subsections of this section describe certain provisions of the Water Purchase 
Agreement.  The following sections constitute summaries of certain provisions of the Agreement 
only and are qualified in all respects by reference to the Agreement itself, a copy of which is 
available from the Village upon request. 
GENERAL PROVISIONS 
In the Water Purchase Agreement, the City agrees to furnish to the Village, and the Village 
agrees to purchase and take from the City a supply of Lake Michigan water through metered 
connections authorized by the Commissioner of the Department of Water of t he City  (the 
“Commissioner”).  All usage of water under the Water Purchase Agreement is to be in accordance 
with the and not in excess of the allocations of Lake Michigan water of the State for each Municipal 
Customer.   
The Water Purchase Agreement authorizes the Village to allocate the water it receives to 
the various Municipal Customers, and further governs the distribution by the Municipal Customers 
to retail customers and, in some cases, other municipal wholesale users.  In addition, the Water 
Purchase Agreement provides that the Village may supply water obtained from the City to certain 
entities in the future, all of which must be located in the State and must have received an allocation 
of water from the State, subject to approval of the Commissioner.  Furthermore, any increase in 
the allocation of water to the Municipal Customers is subject to the approval of the Commissioner.  
The Village is not permitted under the Water Purchase Agreement to furnish water to any party 
outside of its corporate limits, other than the Municipal Customers, without the written consent of 
the Commissioner and the City Council of the City (the “Chicago City Council” ), except that the 
Village may, with the permission of the Commissioner, in an emergency supply water to other  
users of City water that have been previously authorized an emergency connection. 
The Water Purchase Agreement is a continuing valid and binding obligation of the Village 
payable solely from the Revenues. 
Q
UANTITY OF WATER SUPPLIED 
The Water Purchase Agreement provides that the quantity of water to supplied to the 
Village and, subsequently, to the Municipal Customers, is equal to the amount of the full water 
requirements of the Village and the other Municipal Cust omers, subject to the State allocation 
limitations.  The City retains the right to restrict the supply of water in order to ensure that adequate 
water is available to supply all purchasers of City water.   The water provided to the Municipal 
Customers is treated by the City and is of the same qualify as that provided to City residents.  
-20- 
TERM OF THE AGREEMENT 
The Water Purchase Agreement terminates on December 31, 2042, provided that the City 
and the Village may agree to renew the Water Purchase Agreement for one successive term of ten 
years, not to exceed 40 years from the initial date of the Water Purchase Agreement (which was 
February 8, 2013).  If either party desires to renew the Water Purchase Agreement, such party must 
give notice to the other party by June 1 of the year in which the term expires.  The Water Purchase 
Agreement is subject to cancellation if a court of competent jurisdiction restricts or limits any of 
the City’s right to obtain, sell, contract for or distribute Lake Michigan water.    
RATES 
The rates charged to the Village under the Water Purchase Agreement may be no greater 
than the rate fixed for large quantities of water furnished through meters to customers inside the 
City and suburban communities served by the City.  These rates may be adjusted from time -to-
time by an ordinance approved by the Chicago City Council.  These charges may not exceed the 
lowest rate lawfully fixed by the City for water furnished through meters to regular customers.  
The Village may be charged a penalty for late payment of water bills similar to that charged to 
metered customers inside the City.  Payments made by the Village to the City for water under the 
Water Purchase Agreement may be made solely from the Revenues.  The Village covenants and 
agrees with the City in the Water Purchase Agreement to charge such rate or rates for furnishing 
water to its customers so that the Revenues are at all times sufficient to pay in full all amounts due 
to the City from the Village under the Water Purchase Agreement.   
T
HE WATER SALE AGREEMENTS  
The following subsections of this section describe (i) the Regional Water System 
Agreements and (ii) the Southeast Water System  Agreements.  A copy of the Southwest System 
Regional Water Agreement is attached here as APPENDIX C.  Certain information has been redacted 
from the attachment for System security purposes.  The Southeast Water System Agreements are 
available upon request  from the Village.  All discussions of the provisions of such agreements 
herein are qualified in all respects by reference to the Agreements themselves. 
THE REGIONAL WATER AGREEMENTS 
General.  The two Regional Water System Agreements govern the sale of the Lake 
Michigan water purchased by the Village pursuant to the Water Purchase Agreement to the North 
Water System Customers and the Southwest Water System Customers.   
The Southwest System Regional Water Agreement is attached hereto as A
PPENDIX C.  The 
terms of the Regional Water System Agreements pertaining to the Southwest Water System 
Customers and the North Water System Customers are identical except for differences related to 
the project parameters included in such agreements.  
-21- 
The following paragraphs describe certain key provisions of the Regional Water 
Agreements.  Such descriptions are qualified in their entirety by reference to the provisions of the 
Regional Water System Agreements. 
Term of the Regional Water Agreements.  The Regional Water Agreements became 
effective in 2014 and remain in full force for a period of forty years, terminating in the year 2054. 
Take or Pay Contract.  The Regional Water System Agreements are “take or pay” 
contracts.  As such, all capital costs payable or accrued in a given period of the System due under 
the Regional Water System Agreements are due and payable, without setoff or counterclaim and 
irrespective of whether water is ever furnished, made available or delivered to the applicable 
Municipal Customers from the Project or whether any project for the supply of water contemplated 
by the Regional Water System Agreements is completed, operable or operating and 
notwithstanding any suspension, interruption, interference, reduction or curtailment of the supply 
of water from any such project.   
Full Water Requirements.  In the Regional Water Agreements, the Regional Water System 
Customers agree to purchase from the Village, and the Village agrees to sell to the Regional Water 
System Customers, the amount of Chicago Water necessary to serve its Full Water Requirements.  
The Village’s obligation to deliver Chicago Water to each Regional Water System Customer is 
limited (i) to the State Water A llocation of that Regional Water System Customer, and any 
wholesale customer of such Regional Water System Customer, in each Fiscal Year , and (ii) to a 
maximum daily amount not in excess of the Regional Water System Customers Daily Peaking 
Factor.  Regiona l Water System Customers are allowed to seek an alternate source of supply of 
water if the Village is unable to provide the Full Water Requirements of such Regional Water 
System Customer. 
Construction of the 2013 Regional System Improvements.  The Regional Water 
Agreements provide for the construction of the 2013 Regional System Improvements.  
Furthermore, the Regional Water Agreements provide conditions for the issuance of Bonds 
payable from the revenues of the System. 
Customers.  Each Regional  Water System Customer has the exclusive right to serve and 
distribute Chicago Water to (1) is current customers, whether or not within its corporate limits, (ii) 
future customers on land located within its limits at the time of the execution of the Regional Water 
Agreement, (iii) future customers on land lawfully annexed by the System Customer, and (iv) 
future customers not within the corporate limits, subject to the provisions of the Water Purchase 
Agreement.  The Village has the sole and exclusive right to service Municipal Customers and 
Future Water Customers not located within a Municipal Customer’s corporate limits through the 
Regional Water System. 
The Regional Water Agreements also describe the manner in which  other municipalities 
may become Future Water Customers of the System.  The Village may enter into agreements or 
contracts with Future Water Customers other than Southeast System Customers upon receiving 
Corporate Consent Obtained of the Municipal Customers other than the Village having not less 
than 80% of the 2030 State Water Allocations of all of the Municipal Customers of Oak Lawn.  If  
-22- 
the Southeast Water System Customers do not enter into a New Southeast Customer Agreement, 
the Village may enter into agreements or contracts with Southeast System Customers as Future 
Water Customers in the form of a Conforming Agreement.  Southeast System Customers 
becoming Future Water Customers will be required to pay its Proportionate Share of Capital Costs 
and Charges on a take or pay basis, which would then have the effect of reducing the Proportionate 
Shares of all Participating Customers at that time. 
Ownership of the System .  Except as limited by the Regional Water Agreements, the 
ownership of the System is vested in the Village.  Responsibility for the maintenance and repair 
of the System is solely that of the Village.   
Price and Terms of Payments.  In the periods as indicated, each Regional Water System 
Customer must pay to the Village its respective share of the  Aggregate Costs and such other 
amounts due under the Regional Water Agreements.  In each Fiscal Year, the Village provides the 
Regional Water System Customers a summary of the Aggregate Costs of each of the Regional 
Water System Customers.  Payments to the Village by the Regional Water System Customers are 
as follows: 
 A. Operation and Maintenance Costs.  All elements of Operation and Maintenance 
Costs are due and payable monthly and are in default if not paid within thirty (30)  days 
after the due date. 
 (1) Each Regional Water System Customer must pay an amount equal to the 
amount payable by the System to Chicago for the month pursuant to the Water P urchase 
Agreement or any successor agreement for the measured amount of Chicago Water 
delivered by the Village to that Regional Water System Customer at its Point or Points of 
Delivery. 
 (2) Each Regional Water System Customer must pay an amount equal to the 
amount of Electricity Costs incurred for the month by the System times such Re gional 
Water System Customer’s share of such costs as shown in the Regional Water Agreements.  
Except upon Executive Consent Obtained, the rate will not be adjusted during the course 
of a given Fiscal Year from the rate in effect at the start of such Fiscal Year or, if a rate 
increase is known to become effective at the start of such Fiscal Year, then also giving 
effect to such rate increase as of its effective date. 
 (3) Each Municipal Customer must pay an amount equal to the amount of Pump 
Station Maintenance Costs required for the Fiscal Year by the System times such Municipal 
Customer’s share of such costs as shown in the Regional Water Agreements.  Except upon 
Executive Consent Obtained, such amount will not be adjusted during the course of a given 
Fiscal Year. 
 (4) Each Regional Water System Customer pays an amount equal to the amount 
of Transmission Main Maintenance Costs required for the Fiscal Year by the System times 
such Municipal Customer’s share of such costs as shown in the Regional Water  
-23- 
Agreements.  Except upon Executive Consent Obtained, such amount will not be adjusted 
during the course of a given Fiscal Year. 
 (5) Each Municipal Customer pays an amount equal to the System Operations 
Costs required for the Fiscal Year by the Oak Lawn Regional Water System at the Common 
Usage Rate, and, except upon Executive Consent Obtained, such rate will not be adjusted 
during the course of a given Fiscal Year from the rate in effect at the start of such Fiscal 
Year. 
 B. Capital Costs and Charges. All elements of Capital Costs and Charges are  due and 
payable quarterly on the last business day of the months set forth in the Regional Water 
Agreements, and are in default if not paid within thirty (30) days after the due date. 
 (1) Each Regional Water System Customer pays an amount equal to the amount 
of Capital Costs and Charges required for the Fiscal Year by the System as budgeted for 
such Fiscal Year (a)  divided by four to represent a quarterly amount and (b)  times such 
Municipal Customer’s Proportionate Share of such costs.  
 (2) Each Municipal Customer pays  its Default Proportionate Share of Default 
Costs Allocable to Bonds within thirty (30)  days after receipt of notice from the Village 
that such costs are due.  
C. Other Non-Operating Charges.  All elements of Other Non -Operating Charges are 
due and payable monthly and shall be in default thirty (30) days after the due date. 
 (1) Each Municipal Customer pays  an amount equal to the amount of all Other 
Non-Operating Charges required for the Fiscal Year by the System at the Common Usage 
Rate, and, except for payment of Default Costs Allocable to Other Aggregate Costs or upon 
Executive Consent Obtained, are not adjusted during the course of a given Fiscal Year from 
the rate in effect at the start of such Fiscal Year. 
 (2) Other Non-Operating Charges include an accumulation for a reserve for the 
System for Operation and Maintenance Costs (the “O&M Reserve” ) which reserve is 
intended to provide for unforeseen increases in such costs, Default Costs, or, as provided 
in the proceedings for the issuance of the Bonds, to pay Bonds in the event there has been 
a default in the payment of Capital Costs and Charges or to pay Bonds issued in the form 
of a revolving line of credit).  The amount so accumulated for the O&M Reserve shall not 
exceed the sum of (a)  the cost of Chicago Water for the previous Fiscal Year divided by 
12 plus (b)(i) all Operation and Maintenance Costs for the previous Fiscal Year minus said 
cost of Chicago Water for the previous Fiscal Year (ii) divided by 4.  The required amount 
of the O&M Reserve shall be accumulated at the Common Usage Rate of $0.08 (8 cents) 
per 1,000 gallons of Chicago Water delivered times the gallons so delivered.  Draws upon 
said reserve shall be replenished, to the extent required, in the second Fiscal Year after 
such draws.  Increases in the required amount of said reserve  must be fully funded, at a 
Common Usage Rate to be determined, in the two (2) Fiscal Years after the amount of such 
increase is determined.  The accumulation of the O&M Reser ve provided for by this  
-24- 
provision is payable as an Other Non-Operating Charge, but the expenditure of amounts in 
the O&M Reserve will be for specific Operations and Maintenance Costs categories (e.g., 
Chicago Water, Electricity Costs, or Pump Station Maintenance Costs) and Municipal 
Customers and Future Water Customers shall be charged for replenishment on the basis of 
such cost categories pursuant to the True-Up provisions described below. 
Other than as set forth in this section, no Other Non- Operating Charges shall be 
charged by the Oak  Lawn Regional Water System for reserves for Operation and 
Maintenance Costs. 
 (3) Other Non -Operating Charges include an amount in each Fiscal Year 
budgeted to produce an annual contribution (the “Annual Contribution” ) to p rovide 
funding up to full funding ( “Full Funding” ) of the Renewal, Repair and Replacement 
Reserve Fund and thereafter for deposit to the unencumbered reserves of the Oak Lawn 
Regional Water System.  The Annual Contribution amount will equal not less than 
$750,000 for Fiscal Year 2014, and said sum of $750,000 is adjusted for any increase or 
decrease in the PPI in each Fiscal Year thereafter multiplied in each such year by a fraction 
the numerator of which is the amount of Chicago Water delivered through the System to 
Regional Water System  Customers that are paying for the Annual Contribution at the 
Common Usage Rate and the denominator of which is all Chicago Water delivered through 
the System to Regional Water System  Customers.  The Annual Contribution may be 
increased pursuant to the approved Asset Management Program.  Amounts in the Renewal, 
Repair and Replacement Reserve Fund may be expended only for Major Capital Costs or 
System Repairs or, as provided in the proceedings for the issuance of the Bonds, for Default 
Costs, or to pay Bonds in the event there has been a default in the payment of Capital Costs 
and Charges.  Amounts in the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Fund may be 
expended for Major Capital Costs only pursuant to the As set Management Program.  
Amounts to be expended for System Repairs and for Major Capital Costs which in any 
given Fiscal Year are in excess of $1,500,000 must be pursuant to Executive Consent 
Obtained.  Full Funding of the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Fund shall be 
$5,000,000 as measured in Fiscal Year 2014 and said sum of $5,000,000 adjusted for any 
increase or decrease in PPI for each Fiscal Year thereafter.  Full Funding may be increased 
pursuant to the Asset Management Program.  Annual Contributions received at such time 
as the Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Fund is at Full Funding will be retained 
in the unencumbered reserves of the System.  Except in the event amounts provided for the 
Renewal, Repair and Replacement Reserve Fund are expended for Bond payments, the 
Annual Contributions are not subject to the True-Up. 
 (4) Other Non-Operating Charges assessed for insurance reserve purposes will 
only be made pursuant to the report and recommendation of an independent insurance 
consultant having a nationally recognized reputation for competence in such matters and 
specifying both the amount of such reserves as should be reasonably available and the rate 
of accumulation of same. 
 (5) Each Municipal Customer will pay its share of Default Co sts Allocable to 
Other Aggregate Costs, which share shall be as follows:  (a) first, in any given Fiscal Year,  
-25- 
the Village will pay all Default Costs up to an amount equal to the Equitable Return 
received by the Village  for the previous Fiscal Year and (b)  thereafter, all Municipal 
Customers (including the Village) not in default under the Regional Water Agreement will 
pay a share of remaining Default Costs equal to the proportion of Chicago Water delivered 
to such Municipal Customer in the previous Fiscal Year to the Chicago Water delivered to 
all Municipal Customers (including the Village) not in default under this Agreement during 
such Fiscal Year. 
D. Old Bonds Payments.  Each Municipal Customer will pay to the Village the amounts 
due on the Old Bonds at  the times and in the amounts determined as required in the 
Regional Water Agreements. 
 E. True Up.  On an annual basis, each Municipal Customer will receive a statement with
